title: Synthetic example · Improve a monthly evidence-learning huddle
edenaReason: Synthetic, nonclinical learning scenario using public information only; low-consequence and reversible.
summary: Design and evaluate a 20-minute, nonclinical evidence-learning huddle that helps a volunteer team discuss one public article and choose one safe learning action.

assess.notes:
Verified facts: attendance averages four people; the meeting has 20 minutes; only public articles are used.
User-provided information: participants want a predictable format.
Assumptions: one article is manageable.
Unresolved questions: preferred day and accessibility needs.
assess.outcome: A small volunteer learning huddle is feasible if the scope stays nonclinical and the format remains brief.

define.notes:
Central opportunity: make evidence discussion consistent without adding hidden preparation burden.
Contributing factors: changing schedules and unclear facilitation.
Risk: conversation could drift into identifiable cases, which is prohibited.
define.outcome: Create a repeatable, case-free learning huddle using public evidence and a visible stop rule for sensitive discussion.

plan.notes:
Option A: 20-minute live huddle. Option B: asynchronous summary. Option C: alternate both.
Selected: pilot Option A twice.
Measures: attendance, one-question usefulness rating and facilitator preparation time.
Stop rule: stop immediately if anyone introduces identifiable information.
plan.outcome: Run two synthetic/public-evidence huddles with a five-part agenda, named facilitator, anonymous usefulness check and stop rule.

implement.notes: Prepared a one-page agenda, public article link checklist and anonymous feedback question. A human facilitator reviewed the materials. No message was sent and no real meeting was scheduled by this app.
implement.outcome: User-reported outside result: two huddles were held using the reviewed agenda; Mission Control did not execute the action.

evaluate.notes:
Expected: at least three attendees, usefulness at least 4/5 and preparation under 15 minutes.
Observed (synthetic demonstration): four attendees, 4.3/5 usefulness and 12 minutes preparation.
Limitation: demonstration data are illustrative, not measured real-world evidence.
Lesson: keep one article and one action.
evaluate.outcome: Continue for one additional iteration and test rotating facilitators. This is a synthetic example, not evidence of a real outcome.
